CORE.description: Production code that goes to npm package - what users receive
CORE.rule: All source code that makes the framework functional

CONFIG.description: Configuration files required for any development/production environment
CONFIG.rule: Necessary for TypeScript, Git, and build to work

DOCS.description: User-facing documentation for end users and developers using the framework
DOCS.rule: Public-facing guides, API reference, architecture docs

DEVELOPMENT.description: Development-only tools, tests, internal notes, roadmaps - NOT for npm package
DEVELOPMENT.rule: In GitHub repo but EXCLUDED from npm package. For developers only

GENERATED.description: Build artifacts and runtime outputs - should NEVER be committed
GENERATED.rule: Generated at build time. Must be in .gitignore

import_rules.description: Rules for what can import from what. Prevents circular dependencies.
import_rules.CRITICAL: CORE files MUST NEVER import from DEVELOPMENT, DOCS, or GENERATED

file_creation_protocol.description: Before creating ANY new file, follow this protocol to avoid saturation

steps.question: Is this file part of the CORE framework (goes to npm users)?
steps.if_no: This is DEVELOPMENT. Go to step 5

steps.question: Is this CORE file one of:
Does this file only import from CORE and CONFIG?
steps.if_no: STOP. Fix imports or move to DEVELOPMENT

steps.action: Add file path to inventory.CORE.key_files or locations

steps.question: Is this DEVELOPMENT file one of:
options: Development note (_dev/) - Internal docs, roadmaps, analysis

what_is_excluded: dist/ is NOT included as source (generated fresh on install)

monthly_audit_checklist: Are all files in inventory classified correctly?
Are there any 'orphaned' files (not in manifest)?
Are there files in wrong locations?
Are there duplicate files in different locations?
Is any CORE file importing from DEVELOPMENT?
red_flags: More than 15 files in root directory (besides config)
Development notes mixed with production code
Files with unclear purpose (check with Hygienizer Agent)

hygienizer_agent_invocation.description: The Repository Hygienizer Agent is the guardian of this manifest
can_be_invoked_with: /repo-cleanup - Identify misplaced files and suggest relocations
Direct question: 'Where should I put this file?'
hygienizer_agent_invocation.agent_ensures: No file creation without proper classification

metadata.audit_frequency: Every 10 major file additions or weekly
